generated_categories.name: Default Validation
generated_categories.description: Covers baseline email validation with default options and maximum length constraints.

generated_categories.name: Display Name Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests behavior related to display names, including allowing, requiring, and rejecting specific formats or characters.

generated_categories.name: UTF-8 and Local Part Validation
generated_categories.description: Ensures proper handling of UTF-8 characters and quoted local parts in email addresses.

generated_categories.name: TLD and Domain Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests behavior when TLDs are optional, and domain-specific rules such as blacklists, whitelists, and IP domains.

generated_categories.name: Blacklist and Whitelist Validation
generated_categories.description: Validates emails against blacklisted characters or domains and ensures only whitelisted domains are accepted.

generated_categories.name: Domain-Specific Validation
generated_categories.description: Tests validation rules specific to certain domains, such as Gmail and Googlemail.

golden_to_generated.reason: GENC-1 covers baseline email validation with default options and maximum length constraints, which aligns with GC-1's focus on standard email formats and basic rules.
GENC-6 explicitly focuses on domain-specific validation, which directly matches GC-2's description.
No generated category explicitly addresses email validation with underscores in the domain.
GENC-3 focuses on UTF-8 and local part validation, which includes the restriction of UTF-8 characters in the local part as described in GC-4.
GENC-2 covers display name validation, including allowing display names, which aligns with GC-5's focus on optional display names.
GENC-2 covers display name validation, including requiring display names, which aligns with GC-6's focus on mandatory display names.
GENC-4 includes IP domain validation as part of its broader focus on TLD and domain validation, but it does not exclusively focus on IP domain validation as GC-7 does.
GENC-5 includes validation against blacklisted characters, but it also covers domain blacklists and whitelists, making the match partial.
GENC-1 mentions maximum length constraints as part of its baseline validation, but it does not focus exclusively on maximum length restrictions as GC-9 does.
GENC-1 mentions maximum length constraints, which could imply ignoring maximum length, but it does not explicitly focus on ignoring maximum length as GC-10 does.
GENC-5 includes validation against blacklisted domains, but it also covers whitelisted domains and blacklisted characters, making the match partial.
GENC-5 includes validation against blacklisted domains, but it does not explicitly mention the use of regular expressions for blacklisting as GC-12 does.
GENC-5 includes validation for whitelisted domains, but it does not explicitly mention the use of regular expressions for whitelisting as GC-13 does.
GENC-5 includes validation for whitelisted domains, but it does not explicitly mention the use of regular expressions for whitelisting as GC-14 does.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1 covers baseline email validation, which aligns strongly with GC-1, and mentions maximum length constraints, which partially align with GC-9 and GC-10.
GENC-2 focuses on display name validation, which strongly aligns with GC-5 (optional display names) and GC-6 (mandatory display names).
GENC-3 focuses on UTF-8 and local part validation, which strongly aligns with GC-4's restriction on UTF-8 characters in the local part.
GENC-4 includes IP domain validation as part of its broader focus on TLD and domain validation, but it does not exclusively focus on IP domain validation as GC-7 does.

GENC-5 includes validation against blacklisted characters, blacklisted domains, and whitelisted domains, but does not explicitly focus on regular expressions for blacklisting or whitelisting as GC-12, GC-13, and GC-14 do.
GENC-6 explicitly focuses on domain-specific validation, which directly matches GC-2's description.
